qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded list nesting in RLP decoder causes stack overflow DoS via P2P messages
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets unbounded recursive RLP list decoding in rlp/decode.go, while the advisory patch fixes secp256k1 public-coordinate bounds checks in crypto/secp256k1 and crypto/signature_nocgo.go.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying final finding identifies the advisory-described root cause. The advisory references a Geth p2p-message DoS fixed by commit 895a8597cb16c02203e38707ed2d1da5c500fe60, which adds missing secp256k1 coordinate bounds validation. The only qualifying finding instead reports an RLP nested-list stack overflow condition.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-2gjw-fg97-vg3r describes Go Ethereum DoS via malicious p2p message and references fix commit 895a8597cb16c02203e38707ed2d1da5c500fe60.

Fix commit 895a8597cb16c02203e38707ed2d1da5c500fe60 modifies crypto/secp256k1/curve.go, crypto/secp256k1/ext.h, and crypto/signature_nocgo.go to reject coordinates greater than or equal to the secp256k1 field prime.
Finding rlp_stack_overflow_dos identifies rlp/decode.go Stream.List and recursive RLP decoding as the vulnerable path, with p2p/message.go only as a transport path.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 are not applicable.
